description_long: This object is a vintage-style cathode ray tube (CRT) television set, characterized by a boxy, deep body and a convex glass screen. The television is dark gray or black, with a thick bezel surrounding the screen and two prominent metal V-shaped antennas extending upward from the top. It sits atop a simple, light-colored wooden table with four legs, and a black power cord extends from the back of the television, trailing onto the ground. The front panel features a speaker grille and a rotary knob, typical of older television designs. The overall appearance suggests a utilitarian, retro appliance that might be found in an urban garage, sidewalk, or neighborhood living space.
description: A retro CRT television with a convex screen, metal antennas, and a rotary knob, sitting on a light wooden table with a trailing power cord.
description_view_0: Front view showing the convex screen, speaker grille, rotary knob, and both antennas extending upward.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the deep, boxy back of the television and the power cord trailing from the rear.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back panel, both antennas, and the power cord extending downward.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the depth of the television and the power cord, with the table's legs clearly visible.
attribute_bezel_design: thick, black or dark gray plastic bezel with a utilitarian, retro style
